title: narcissus triandrus var. nutans
intro: Narcissus triandrus var. nutans, commonly known as the Lily-of-the-North, is a bulbous perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbalism, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its alkaloid content, which has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Narcissus triandrus var. nutans include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
